rationale: Both papers center on tariff-choice/flat-rate bias (citing: “Tariff Choice with Consumer Learning and Switching Costs”; cited: “Paying Too Much and Being Happy about It…Tariff-Choice Biases”), and the cited note provides core prior evidence on the existence/causes/consequences of those biases. The citing note explicitly positions itself against prior tariff-choice explanations (behavioral misjudgment vs. rational learning), so this citation is plausibly foundational or a direct comparison point rather than an incidental mention.
